opp.ais_why:
• United Airlines' team flagged a direct AI-adjacent mandate — needing intake beyond tactical requests with intelligent triage and routing.
• Rep is positioning ANA as the wedge by spinning the story back to ANA and intake — Zycus' agentic intake is the chosen entry point.
• Cadence is weak: champion Aashima moved to American Airlines, the COE has gone silent, and last activity was April 2026 — AI interest exists but no active champion.

sources: SF Q3 SOQL: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all null
Avoma opp+account discovery: zero buyer calls matched
SF Next_Step__c (last updated context from Jan 2026): 'spin the story back to ANA and intake'
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case documented across Salesforce or Avoma. The Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c fields returned no data in Q3 SOQL. Zero buyer calls discovered via opp+account Avoma pull, so no verbatim pain articulation exists. The Next Step field references 'spin the story back to ANA and intake' and 'Need to spin the story back to ANA and intake', implying a use-case narrative around airline network analytics and intake/request management, but this is rep-side positioning, not buyer-stated pain. Without a quantified financial or operational impact (cost savings, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement, headcount efficiency), the $100k investment lacks an approval anchor for CFO Mike Leskinen or CPO Bhav Rai. The gap is a knowledge gap: the pain exists (United is evaluating intake/procurement solutions across six vendors), but Zycus has not extracted or documented the quantified case. Immediate move: secure a discovery call with Nader Padidar or Vandana Parab to quantify current intake process cost (manual effort, cycle time, maverick spend) and build a defensible ROI model before any formal eval can proceed.

champion.narrative: No active champion post-Aashima Jain's departure. Aashima Jain, Director of the Global Procurement Transformation Office, was listed as Executive Sponsor in contact roles and was the original internal advocate per Next Step context ('Aashima moving to American', 'connect with her via LinkedIn to get recommendation / support for UA internal navigation (In Process 27th Jan)', 'Social Connection with Aashima (Who has gone quiet since Horizon) - January Lunch?'). Her departure to a competitor (referenced as 'American' in Next Step, likely American Airlines) in early 2026 created a champion void. The Next Step references intent to 'leverage up to Aashima replacement', but no replacement is named or mapped in contact roles. The remaining contacts are evaluators (Nader Padidar, Evaluator role; Vandana Parab, Influencer role; Rachel Clark, Manager; Anson Best, Director) with no documented advocacy evidence. The Next Step shows relationship-building intent ('Vandana - LinkedIn Connect over the holiday break', 'Rachel - Outreach for New Years', 'Anson Best has agreed to attend the Chicago roundtable April 8th'), but these are early-stage social touches, not championship. The X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c fields are null per Q3 SOQL. Zero buyer calls mean no evidence of internal advocacy (introductions, shared docs, competitive push-back, agenda-driving). Without a champion, Zycus has no internal navigator to drive the eval process, no intel on the competitive field, and no advocate to escalate to the CFO/CPO. Immediate move: prioritize building Vandana Parab or Nader Padidar into a champion by delivering unique value (competitive intel, ROI model, exec access) and securing their commitment to drive the eval internally and provide visibility into the decision process.

sources: SF Competitors__c: Coupa;Cobblestone;Ironclad;Agiloft;Conga
SF Next_Step__c: 'Zoominfo says UA is looking at iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive'
SF Q3 SOQL: Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c all null
Avoma: zero buyer calls, no competitive intel captured
competition.narrative: Five named competitors in Salesforce Competitors__c field: Coupa, Cobblestone, Ironclad, Agiloft, Conga. The Next Step adds a sixth: iValua, noted as 'Zoominfo says UA is looking at iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive', positioning iValua as a strong fit for United's on-prem preference and MRO procurement depth. Coupa is listed first in the competitor string and is the most likely primary threat given its market dominance in intake/procurement and airline-sector penetration. The Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c fields are all null per Q3 SOQL, so there is no documented competitive differentiation narrative, no intel on which vendors are leading, and no Zycus wedge articulated. Zero buyer calls mean no verbatim competitive mentions or buyer sentiment captured. The multi-vendor field (six competitors) signals a broad, early-stage evaluation with no shortlist yet. Without competitive intel or a delivered wedge, Zycus is one of seven vendors with no differentiation story in front of the buyer. The wedge: Zycus's strength is Merlin Intake (agentic AI for request automation) and iContract (CLM depth), positioning against Coupa's workflow rigidity and iValua's dated UX. The activation: make Merlin Intake the Phase 2 AI narrative centerpiece and contrast it with Coupa's lack of agentic automation, delivered in the next buyer call with a live demo and ROI model showing intake cycle-time reduction and error elimination.

sources: SF Q3 SOQL: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c all null

identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain United is solving remains undocumented and unquoted. Zero buyer calls were discovered via opp+account Avoma pull, so no verbatim buyer articulation of pain exists. The Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c fields are all null per Q3 SOQL. The Products__c field lists 'iContract;iRequest;Merlin;Merlin Intake;Agentic AI', implying United's pain centers on intake/request management (manual, slow, maverick-prone) and possibly contract lifecycle inefficiency, but this is inferred from product scope, not stated by the buyer. The Next Step references 'spin the story back to ANA and intake', suggesting the pain narrative Zycus is positioning is around airline network analytics (ANA) and procurement intake automation, but again this is rep-side positioning, not buyer confirmation. The How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c and Existing_vendor__c fields are null, so the current-state pain is unknown. The compelling event is also unstated (What_if_this_is_not_done__c null). Without a named pain owner or a confirmed operational/financial impact, the deal lacks urgency and a business case anchor. Immediate move: in the re-engagement call, ask Nader or Vandana to describe the current intake process pain (manual effort, cycle time, error rate, user frustration, maverick spend), quantify it, and confirm who owns fixing it (likely the CPO or the COE lead replacing Aashima).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec process documented. The Mandate__c field is null per Q3 SOQL. There is no mention of MSA, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec requirements in the Next Step narrative or any Salesforce free-text field. The Current_Contract_Expiration__c field is null, so there is no incumbent contract forcing function to anchor a timeline. No SI or partner involvement is documented (no Partner account association, no partner mentioned in Next Step). The Next Step references 'Multithread to IT', implying IT stakeholders will be involved (likely for ERP/systems integration and InfoSec review given United's scale), but no IT contacts are in the contact roles, and no IT engagement is recorded. At this stage (Qualified, 354 days in), paper-process visibility is not yet expected, but the lack of any IT or procurement-channel intel is a risk given the deal is 224 days from close. Immediate move: in the next call, ask Nader or Vandana who owns IT approval, whether InfoSec review is required, and what the standard procurement/contracting timeline is at United for a $100k SaaS purchase.

However, there is zero evidence of his active engagement: no Avoma calls with his attendance, no Salesforce tasks or events referencing him, and no mention in the Next Step narrative. The Next Step from Jan 2026 references 'Multithread to IT and up to the relevant stakeholder', implying an intent to escalate to the CPO (Bhav Rai, the Decision Maker contact role), but no follow-through is documented. The EB role is identified on paper but entirely passive. Without direct CFO engagement or a quantified business case to present to him, budget approval is speculative. The Who_will_approve_budget__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c fields were not populated in Q3 SOQL. decision_process.narrative: The contact role map and Next Step narrative imply a multi-layered approval process but no formalized timeline or sequence. The org chart shows CFO Mike Leskinen and CPO Bhav Rai at the executive layer (both Executive Sponsor and Decision Maker roles respectively), with a mid-tier evaluation team: Nader Padidar (Sr. Manager Procurement Strategy, Evaluator role), Vandana Parab (Senior Manager Procurement Analytics, Influencer role), Anson Best (Director Global Procurement), and Rachel Clark (Manager Procurement Process). The departed Executive Sponsor Aashima Jain (Director, Global Procurement Transformation Office) was the original process owner per Next Step context ('Silence from the relevant stakeholder (possible quiet chaos with Aashima leaving)'). The What_is_the_decision_process__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c fields are null in Q3 SOQL. The Next Step references event-based engagement ('Rachel Cark from the COE is interested in attending the Chicago Roundtable April 8th', 'Anson Best has agreed to attend the Chicago roundtable April 8th'), suggesting informal relationship-building rather than a structured vendor evaluation process. No RFI, RFP, or shortlist stage is documented in Salesforce submission-date fields. The approval chain is inferred as: eval team (Nader, Vandana, Rachel, Anson) → CPO Bhav Rai → CFO Mike Leskinen, but this has never been confirmed on a call. The Multiple_approvals__c and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c fields are null. Immediate move: in the next buyer conversation, map the formal decision process, approval gates, and timeline explicitly.

decision_criteria.narrative: No structured evaluation criteria documented. The Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields returned null in Q3 SOQL. Zero buyer calls mean no use-case validation, test-case sessions, or RFP/RFI rounds have been transcribed or recorded. The Next Step references a broad product scope (iContract, iRequest, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI per SF Products__c), but there is no evidence the buyer has articulated how they will evaluate vendors on these capabilities, nor any scoring framework or decision rubric. The Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c field is also null. The only inferred criterion is that United is MRO-intensive (per Next Step: 'iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive'), suggesting on-prem deployment and MRO procurement depth matter, but this is rep inference from ZoomInfo, not buyer confirmation. Without documented criteria, Zycus cannot tailor its positioning or measure its competitive standing. Immediate move: in the first re-engagement call with Nader Padidar or Vandana Parab, ask directly what the evaluation scorecard is, what weight intake/AI capabilities carry, and whether MRO depth or on-prem options are formal requirements.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit is entirely speculative. The AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c fields are null, so there is no field-based AI excitement read. The AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c field is null. Zero buyer calls mean no verbatim AI appetite articulation. The product scope includes Merlin and Agentic AI, suggesting the rep believes AI is in scope, but this is unconfirmed by the buyer. United's profile (large enterprise, MRO-intensive, procurement transformation office per Aashima's title) implies organizational sophistication that could support AI adoption, but without a documented business case, champion, or confirmed eval criteria, AI appetite is unknown. The tier is AI Curious by default (product scope suggests interest, but no validation), not AI Hungry (no compelling event, no exec sponsorship, no urgency) or AI Resistant (no explicit objection documented).

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Aashima Jain Executive Sponsor departed), SF Next_Step__c (relationship-building intent, no advocacy evidence), Avoma zero buyer calls
champion_strength.summary: No active champion. Aashima Jain, the original Executive Sponsor and internal advocate, departed to a competitor (noted as 'moving to American' in Next Step, early 2026), creating a void. The remaining contacts (Nader Padidar, Vandana Parab, Anson Best, Rachel Clark) are in relationship-building stage with no documented advocacy behavior (no introductions, shared internal docs, competitive push-back, or agenda-driving). The Next Step narrative shows intent to develop Vandana and Nader as champion candidates ('LinkedIn Connect', 'informal meet', '1:1 on 7th, 8th or 9th'), but execution is pending and no two-way engagement is documented. Without a champion, Zycus lacks internal navigation, competitive intel, and an advocate to drive the eval process and escalate to the CFO/CPO.

items.action: Secure a discovery call with Nader Padidar or Vandana Parab within 7 days to quantify current intake process pain (manual effort, cycle time, error rate, maverick spend), build a defensible ROI model showing cost savings and efficiency gains, and confirm the evaluation scorecard and timeline. This is the foundation for all subsequent moves: without a quantified business case, CFO approval is speculative, and without confirmed decision criteria, competitive positioning cannot be tailored.

north_star_verdict.headline: 354 days in Qualified with no formal evaluation, no champion post-Aashima departure, 5-vendor competitive field, and 224 days to close demanding immediate buyer commitment that has not materialized

flags: No active champion: Aashima Jain (Executive Sponsor) departed; no replacement mapped; remaining contacts are evaluators with no advocacy evidence
Economic Buyer Mike Leskinen (CFO) entirely passive: zero calls, tasks, or engagement documented; no path to budget approval without CFO sponsorship

best_practice_check.summary: Multiple critical gaps blocking progression from Qualified to formal evaluation after 354 days. No champion, no EB engagement, no quantified business case, no competitive wedge delivered, and no confirmed decision criteria. The deal is relationship-building only with no structured eval process.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of seven vendors (Coupa, iValua, Cobblestone, Ironclad, Agiloft, Conga, Zycus) in a broad, early-stage evaluation with no shortlist, no documented competitive wedge delivered to the buyer, and no intel on vendor rankings or buyer sentiment. Coupa and iValua are the likely primary threats: Coupa for market dominance in procurement/intake, iValua for fit with United's on-prem preference and MRO depth per ZoomInfo intel. Zycus has relationship-building touches with mid-tier evaluators (Nader, Vandana, Rachel, Anson) but no champion, no exec engagement, and no differentiation narrative in front of the buyer. Without a delivered competitive story and no buyer calls to capture sentiment, Zycus's position is undifferentiated and at risk of elimination in any shortlist cut.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: Zoominfo says UA is looking at iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is product-scope presence only (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI in SF Products__c), with no evidence of the narrative landing with the buyer. The AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c fields are null, so there is no AI excitement signal from the field or from any AI-needs assessment. The AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c field is also null. Zero buyer calls mean no Merlin demo, no agentic-AI value discussion, and no AI appetite confirmation. The Next Step references 'spin the story back to ANA and intake', suggesting the rep is positioning Merlin Intake as part of an airline-network-analytics + procurement-intake narrative, but there is no evidence this has been delivered or resonated. Without a delivered AI story and no buyer validation of AI appetite, Merlin is at risk of being deprioritized or cut from scope in favor of baseline workflow automation (where Coupa and iValua compete). Under-positioned.

items.source: SF Products__c: iRequest, Merlin Intake (inferred criterion from product scope, not buyer-stated)
SF Products__c: iContract (inferred criterion from product scope, not buyer-stated)

items.quote: iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive
items.source: SF Next_Step__c (rep inference from ZoomInfo, positioning iValua as strong fit for MRO; Zycus MRO depth not documented or delivered to buyer)

items.quote: iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive
items.source: SF Next_Step__c (rep inference from ZoomInfo, suggests United may prefer on-prem; Zycus is cloud-native SaaS-first, no on-prem option documented)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations are inferred from product scope (iContract, iRequest, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI per SF Products__c) but never confirmed on a buyer call or documented in requirements fields. The inferred expectation is a unified intake-to-contract solution with AI-driven automation for request routing, contract lifecycle management, and procurement process optimization, likely targeting manual-effort reduction, cycle-time improvement, and maverick-spend control. The MRO-intensive context (inferred from Next Step: 'iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive') suggests United may prioritize MRO procurement depth and possibly on-prem deployment, areas where Zycus's cloud-native, SaaS-first architecture may be exposed vs iValua. Without documented decision criteria, use-case validation, or buyer articulation of priorities, fit is speculative. The AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c field is null, so AI appetite is unconfirmed despite Merlin being in product scope. Immediate move: in the next call, confirm the eval scorecard, ask whether on-prem is a requirement (and if so, whether cloud+on-prem hybrid is acceptable), and validate AI/Merlin interest level vs purely workflow automation.

hard.ais_why:
• United Airlines' team flagged a direct AI-adjacent mandate — needing intake beyond tactical requests with intelligent triage and routing.
• Rep is positioning ANA as the wedge by spinning the story back to ANA and intake — Zycus' agentic intake is the chosen entry point.
• Cadence is weak: champion Aashima moved to American Airlines, the COE has gone silent, and last activity was April 2026 — AI interest exists but no active champion.

value.source: SF Competitors__c field
value.how_we_win: Merlin Intake agentic AI for request automation (eliminate manual intake routing, 50%+ cycle-time reduction) vs Coupa's rigid workflow engine; iContract CLM depth vs Coupa's lightweight contract module; position Merlin as Phase 2 AI transformation centerpiece in next buyer call with live demo and quantified ROI

value.name: iValua
value.quote: Zoominfo says UA is looking at iValua - would make sense for a. on prem and b. MRO intensive
value.source: SF Next_Step__c (ZoomInfo intel, not buyer confirmation)
value.how_we_win: Cloud-native agility and Merlin AI vs iValua's on-prem legacy and dated UX; position Zycus as the modern, AI-first alternative that delivers MRO depth without on-prem lock-in, with faster time-to-value

value.name: Cobblestone
value.source: SF Competitors__c field
value.how_we_win: Enterprise scale and AI depth (Merlin) vs Cobblestone's mid-market CLM focus; position Zycus as the only vendor delivering integrated intake + contract + AI at United's complexity and volume

value.name: Ironclad
value.source: SF Competitors__c field
value.how_we_win: Procurement-centric CLM (iContract) vs Ironclad's legal-centric workflow; position Zycus as the only solution that unifies procurement intake (iRequest/Merlin Intake) with contract execution, eliminating handoff friction

value.name: Agiloft
value.source: SF Competitors__c field
value.how_we_win: Out-of-box airline/procurement best practices vs Agiloft's low-code DIY complexity; position Zycus as faster to deploy, lower TCO, and AI-augmented vs Agiloft's manual configuration burden

value.name: Conga
value.source: SF Competitors__c field
value.how_we_win: Procurement-native platform vs Conga's document-generation bolt-on to Salesforce; position Zycus as a unified source-to-contract suite with AI vs Conga's fragmented, Salesforce-dependent architecture
